title: oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba
intro: Oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba, commonly known as the pointed-leaf wood sorrel, is a plant species in the Oxalidaceae family. It is known for its potential health benefits, which include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The therapeutic actions of Oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. However, I couldn't confirm the bioactive constituents of this plant species.

These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, improve heart health by reducing cholesterol levels, and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause infections. parts: Oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of this plant contain iridoids, including aucubin, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems. The roots of Oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba contain glycosides, including saponins, which have been used to treat fever and other systemic conditions.
side_effects: Oxalis bifurca var. angustiloba can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting, due to its potential toxicity when ingested in large quantities.
